summary: Set 075 Session 2 of 2 (FINAL) -- Consumer enablement + deferred-repo record + close. Ran on claude-opus-4-8 (operator-started; the routed next-SESSION rec had advised google/gemini-flash for routed-call cost but carried an explicit operator caveat that S2's careful cross-repo INSTRUCTION-FILE edits may want a tier-2 orchestrator -- operator chose Claude). A protocol-ROLLOUT + cross-repo-ENABLEMENT session: NO ai_router code changed, NO version bump, NO PyPI/Marketplace release (by design). DELIVERED: (1) ENABLED the two source-bearing pilot repos, EACH committed+pushed in its OWN repo: dabbler-platform (LEAD, source-dominated) -- requirements.txt router pin >=0.1.0 -> >=0.26.0 + greenfield-matrix-addendum wired into CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md (commit aac874e); dabbler-access-harvester (SUPPORTING, packaging-small/confounded) -- pin >=0.15.0 -> >=0.26.0 + addendum wired in (commit a70c023). For BOTH, the venv was actually upgraded (platform 0.10.0->0.26.1, harvester 0.18.0->0.26.1) and the matrix CLI (python -m ai_router.verification_only_app run) confirmed importable + parsing the addendum's exact args (--target/--base/--head/--cell SURFACE:PROVIDER[:MODEL]/--orchestrator-provider/--orchestrator-model/--out) -- this gap (pin bump alone left the venvs on pre-0.26 versions that lacked the entrypoint) originated lesson L-075-1. (2) RECORDED the deferral of dabbler-access-migration-orchestrator (doc-only, excluded from the finding-power pool; optional pull-only sidecar only, tagged diffClass=docs-only-excluded/includedInFindingPower=false) -- a one-line note in its CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md (commit f7547a4; disambiguated in 7856089 after the verifier Minor). (3) Added SECTION 10 to docs/verification-surface-strategy.md (routed draft via route(documentation, gemini); pilot is live; measures relative finding yield+precision vs the adjudicated union NOT recall; cohort = platform lead / harvester supporting / migration-orchestrator deferred; the two validity threats+mitigations; ALL held decisions -- live default pull provider, RETIRE -- stay held; matrix is still not RETIRE evidence; synthesis is a future set). (4) change-log.md (both sessions). (5) Lesson L-075-1 (a dependency-pin bump is not enablement until the target venv is upgraded AND the entrypoint confirmed). (6) Routed next-SESSION-SET recommendation (route(analysis) -> google, cross-provider from the claude orchestrator per L-064-6; saved raw to s2-next-set.md): primary 076-seeded-recall-calibration-lane (the fast-follow NOT gated on consumer telemetry, runs in parallel; carries its own design-approval prerequisite), fallback WAIT then open the greenfield finding-power synthesis set once its data gate is met. VERIFICATION: routed_gate REQUIRED (breadth: 4 files); cross-provider session-verification by openai/gpt-5.4 (cross-provider from the anthropic orchestrator) returned VERIFIED with 2 Minors (non-blocking per L-071-1) -- BOTH fixed anyway as cheap accuracy improvements: (a) change-log 'un-gated' wording reworded to 'not gated on consumer telemetry (carries its own design-approval prerequisite)'; (b) the migration-orchestrator '§10' citation disambiguated to the protocol's OWN §5/§10 (the protocol doc genuinely has a §10 Pilot Cohort, so the citation was accurate-but-ambiguous; verified in-doc before acting per the don't-dismiss-without-repro discipline). No re-verify round (Minor-only, L-071-1). s2-verification.md saved raw, never edited. DOGFOOD (pathAwareCritique: advisory, openai+google, L-070-1): google VERIFIED/0 findings; openai 1 Major = the EXPECTED end-of-set ORDERING artifact -- the dogfood ran BEFORE close_session, so change-log/SECTION10 narrate closure while session-state.json still showed S2 in-progress; resolved by this close (close_session flips state->complete + writes events + this final disposition in the same close sequence). NOT a new content defect; converged per L-070-1 (no new code change), committed as the pre-close gate artifact -- no clean re-run chased.

cross_repo_commits.dabbler-platform: aac874e (LEAD enablement: router pin >=0.26.0 + addendum wiring)
cross_repo_commits.dabbler-access-harvester: a70c023 (supporting enablement: router pin >=0.26.0 + addendum wiring)

reason.specifics: FINAL session of Set 075 -- there is no next session in this set. The routed next-SESSION-SET recommendation (route(task_type=analysis) -> google, cross-provider from the claude orchestrator per L-064-6; saved raw to s2-next-set.md) recommends opening 076-seeded-recall-calibration-lane as the next canonical set (the fast-follow NOT gated on consumer telemetry accumulation; carries its own design-approval prerequisite -- the seeded-recall experimental design + initial defect corpus), with fallback WAIT-then-greenfield-finding-power-synthesis once consumer matrix runs accumulate telemetry. That set is mostly protocol/harness authoring (medium complexity); the engine/effort here is the routed cost-optimal pick for its routed analysis load, but the operator selects the next-set orchestrator at start as usual. Cross-provider session-verification stays cross-provider regardless.
